summaryText: Guest speaker Myron Golden opened the Kingdom Impact series by exploring the foundational truths of Genesis 1. He taught that the first thing God reveals about Himself is His creative nature, and because humanity is made in God's image, we are also created to be creative. The sermon centered on the first command God gave humanity: to be fruitful, multiply, and have dominion. Pastor Myron explained this as a 'Be, Do, Have' principle, where our identity in God (Be) determines our activity (Do), which leads to the blessings we steward (Have). He emphasized that true fulfillment and kingdom impact come from saturating our lives with God's Word, discovering the unique creative assignment God has planted within us, and using it to serve others. The message provided a practical framework for living a purpose-driven life, connecting our daily work to God's original design for humanity to rule and create as His representatives on earth.
mainPoints: Humanity is made in the image of a creative God and is therefore designed to create and have dominion as His representatives.
God's first command establishes a 'Be, Do, Have' principle: our God-given identity determines our activity, which leads to our stewardship and blessings.
Fulfillment and kingdom impact are found by saturating our lives with God's Word, discovering our unique assignment, and using it to serve others through creation, connection, and contribution.

versesMentioned.text: Then God said, 'Let us make mankind in our image, in our likeness, so that they may rule over the fish in the sea and the birds in the sky, over the livestock and all the wild animals, and over all the creatures that move along the ground.'

So God created mankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them; male and female he created them. God blessed them and said to them, 'Be fruitful and increase in number; fill the earth and subdue it. Rule over the fish in the sea and the birds in the sky and over every living creature that moves on the ground.'
In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked or stand in the way that sinners take or sit in the company of mockers, but whose delight is in the law of the LORD, and who meditates on his law day and night. That person is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season and whose leaf does not wither—whatever they do prospers.
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.

'For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways,' declares the LORD. 'As the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways and my thoughts than your thoughts. As the rain and the snow come down from heaven, and do not return to it without watering the earth and making it bud and flourish, so that it yields seed for the sower and bread for the eater, so is my word that goes out from my mouth: It will not return to me empty, but will accomplish what I desire and achieve the purpose for which I sent it.'
Keep this Book of the Law always on your lips; meditate on it day and night, so that you may be careful to do everything written in it. Then you will be prosperous and successful.

Monday.scripture: Genesis 1:27 - 'So God created mankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them; male and female he created them.'
Monday.action: Write down three truths about who you are because you are made in God's image.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for creating me in Your image. Help me to see myself the way You see me today.

Tuesday.scripture: Genesis 1:28 - 'God blessed them and said to them, "Be fruitful and increase in number; fill the earth and subdue it. Rule over..."'
Tuesday.action: Identify one area where you need to 'become more' (grow in character or skill) in order to 'do more' in your assignment.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, show me the next step in my personal growth so I can better fulfill the assignment You have for me.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 1:2-3 - '...but whose delight is in the law of the LORD, and who meditates on his law day and night. That person is like a tree planted by streams of water...'
Wednesday.action: Spend 10 minutes meditating on one verse from this week's scriptures. Write down what it reveals about God's character.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, saturate my heart and mind with Your Word. Let it take root and produce good fruit in my life.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 - 'But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.'
Thursday.action: Evaluate your schedule or budget. Make one practical decision that puts God's kingdom purposes ahead of a personal desire.
Thursday.prayer: King Jesus, I yield my plans and priorities to You today. Be the sovereign ruler over every area of my life.

Friday.scripture: Isaiah 55:10-11 - 'As the rain and the snow come down from heaven... so is my word that goes out from my mouth...'
Friday.action: Share a scripture or biblical truth that has encouraged you this week with one other person.
Friday.prayer: God, let Your Word accomplish its purpose in me and through me as I share it with others.

Saturday.scripture: Joshua 1:8 - '...meditate on it day and night, so that you may be careful to do everything written in it. Then you will be prosperous and successful.'
Saturday.action: Review the main points from the sermon. Choose one 'do' action from the week and commit to practicing it in the coming week.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, thank You for Your practical Word. Help me to be a doer and not just a hearer, trusting You for prosperity in my assignment.
